results.title: ngarinyin pathways dulwan
results.material_description: Multi-channel installation, video, projections, text, audio, bespoke screens, tiered  wooden seating , timber and LED strips
results.brief_description:
A collaboration between artist-filmmaker Jeff Doring and the Ngarinyin Aboriginal Corporation. This spatial installation provides access and insight into the art and culture of the Ngarinyin people. The intention is to record and interpret a broad spectrum of Ngarinyin knowledge and law. The work consists of five discrete and interrelated elements:
1. The Introduction zone: a space consisting of a text, audio, visual introduction to the installation.
2. The Water and Wanjina zone: a corridor with apertures that open on the side to reveal visual works.
3. The Man Blood Law zone: a three screen installation with tiered seating that lies at the end of the Water and Wanjina corridor.
4. The Cave zone: an exhibition space which spatially represents a cave with images projected inside it.
5. The Today zone: a space which provides a contemporary perspective on the exhibition via a web installation.

results.title: The Devil's Playground
results.creator_credit: Fred Schepisi and Feature Film House Productions
results.brief_description: The Devil’s Playground is a 1976 Australian drama film written, produced and directed by Fred Schepisi. It is a semi-autobiographical film which tells the story of a boy growing up and going to school in a Catholic juniorate administered by De La Salle Brothers.

constellations_other.name: Fred Schepisi
constellations_other.description: Fred Schepisi's diverse body of work connects to acclaimed local productions, westerns and Hollywood favourites.

results.creator_credit_for_label: Fred Schepisi and Feature Film House Productions
results.description: The subject of a Catholic adolescence in a preparatory school for seminarians set in provincial Victoria, 1953, is substantially autobiographical which explains the engaging intimacy that Schepisi achieves in his first film. An unusual feature, given its subject, is the avoidance of a single point of view. It is not just the youths that are trapped by the Irish Catholic rigidities of the institution but the staff as well. The film does not question belief so much as the institutional distortions that are engendered by the imperatives of belief. Cast includes Arthur Dignam, Nick Tate, Simon Burke and Thomas Keneally.

results.title: Malcolm
results.brief_description: Cascade Films’ Malcolm (1986) follows a shy man (Colin Friels) with a genius for invention. After being sacked from the tram depot for building his own locomotive, he meets Judy (Lindy Davies) and Frank (John Hargreaves), two would-be bank robbers.

constellations_primary.name: Quirky, eccentric, charming
constellations_primary.description: The eponymous character of Malcolm (1986) is a kind and gentle young man obsessed with trams and making gadgets. Such charming and eccentric figures have a long lineage in screen history.

results.description:
In 1986, the same year that Crocodile Dundee stormed the US box office, a much quirkier film was also making international waves. Cascade Films’ Malcolm (1986) follows a shy man (Colin Friels) with a genius for invention. After being sacked from the tram depot for building his own locomotive, he meets Judy (Lindy Davies) and Frank (John Hargreaves), two would-be bank robbers. The trio use Malcolm’s gadgets to pull off the heist of the century, forming an unlikely family in the process.

The first film from director/writer duo Nadia Tass and David Parker, Malcolm was followed by many more films by the pair, including Rikky and Pete (1988), The Big Steal (1990), and Amy (1997).

Malcolm won eight AFI awards and received international acclaim.

results.title: Suzi's story
results.creator_credit: Iain Gillespie, Carlyon-Gillespie Production and Pro-image
results.brief_description: This is the true story of the Lovegrove family. It is the story of Suzi, a loving wife and mother stricken with AIDS. This documentary poignantly deals with this tragic and deadly virus, and seeks to educate the viewer about the debilitating effects of AIDS not only on the victim, but on those who love her. This film sensitively brings home the reality of AIDS. It won’t easily be forgotten.

results.title: Blackmagic Design Media Preservation Lab
results.brief_description: The Media Preservation Lab is a purposed built public facing lab.
results.description: There are about 250,000 artefacts in the ACMI Collection. We’ve been collecting film, videogames, artworks, home movies, stills and press kits – as well as a huge range of other paraphernalia – for over 75 years, ever since ACMI was conceived as a film lending and education organisation known as the State Film Centre. Inside the Blackmagic Media Preservation Lab, our staff and collaborators are digitising our film and time-based media collections, assessing physical artefacts from our videogame collection and film donations, as well as conducting hours of quality assessment work on our born-digital works to make the collection accessible onsite and online.
description_for_label: There are about 250,000 artefacts in the ACMI Collection, which we’ve been collecting for over 75 years – ever since ACMI was conceived as a film lending and education organisation called the State Film Centre. In the Blackmagic Media Preservation Lab, we’re digitising our film and time-based media collections, assessing physical artefacts from our videogame collection and film donations, as well as making the collection accessible onsite and online.

results.title: What's Your Angle?
results.brief_description:
A 2-player cooperative puzzle-platformer with a unique twist: asymmetrical gameplay. Unlike typical co-op games where players have similar roles, here each player experiences the game from a different gravity direction and faces unique obstacles. Despite their differences, both players contribute equally to solving the puzzles.
Each character has a unique gravity (e.g., one sees the world top-down, the other as a side-view platformer), creating drastically different experiences in the same level layout.
Players must collaborate, using character-specific abilities to assist each other and solve puzzles.
Unlike games like Toodee and Topdee, which split the perspectives, this game lets both gravity perspectives exist at the same time, adding complexity and creativity to the puzzles.

results.title: The Woods
results.credit_line:
Candice Breitz
Multi-channel installation, video, colour and audio
Commissioned by ACMI + PEM (Salem)
results.material_description: Multi-channel installation, video, colour and audio
results.brief_description: The Woods is a video installation comprised of three documentaries on child film stars from the United States of America, Nigeria and India. ‘The Audition’ features Hollywood stars, ‘The Interview’ features Nollywood stars, and ‘The Rehearsal’ features Bollywood stars. The Woods was exhibited at ACMI as part of The Character exhibition in 2015.
